## Model catalog (`parsnip`)

- **`parsnip`** names the **model family**; **`set_engine()`** picks the R implementation (`"rpart"`, `"ranger"`, `"xgboost"`, `"glm"`, `"nnet"`, …).
- **`tune()`** marks hyperparameters you will search later with **`tune_grid()`**.
- **Discover arguments:** `?rand_forest`, then in R: `extract_parameter_set_dials(mod_spec)`.

| Model spec | Typical tuned knobs | Common engine |
|------------|---------------------|---------------|
| `decision_tree()` | `tree_depth`, `min_n`, `cost_complexity` | `rpart` |
| `rand_forest()` | `mtry`, `min_n`, `trees` | `ranger` |
| `boost_tree()` | `trees`, `tree_depth`, `learn_rate` | `xgboost` |
| `logistic_reg()` | `penalty` (glmnet) | `glm` / `glmnet` |
| `mlp()` | `hidden_units`, `penalty`, `epochs` | `nnet` / `keras` |

Official lists: [tidymodels — find models](https://www.tidymodels.org/find/parsnip/) · [Engines at a glance](https://www.tidymodels.org/find/parsnip/#engines-at-a-glance).

More depth: [Module 05 — Advanced models](../modules/module-05-advanced-models.qmd) · [Module 08 — Compare learners](../modules/module-08-penguins-compare-models.qmd).

```{r}
#| label: day04-catalog-dials
#| echo: true
#| message: false
rf_spec <- rand_forest(trees = tune(), mtry = tune()) |>
  set_engine("ranger") |>
  set_mode("classification")
extract_parameter_set_dials(rf_spec)
```
